steps.title: Observe the equation form
steps.content: The equation $y = 16 + 14(x - 1)$ contains two additive terms: 16 and $14(x - 1)$.

steps.title: Apply commutativity to match a choice
steps.content:
Rearrange the terms using commutativity of addition: $$16+14(x-1)=14(x-1)+16.$$ That matches Choice C — $y = 14(x - 1) + 16$ exactly — C.

This is the same equation because addition can be done in either order; the coefficient, variable expression, and constant term all stay unchanged.
